item.title: from a Trump supporter: ‘Why do liberals think Trump supporters are stupid?’
item.description: [EDIT TO FIX LINKS] An anguished question from a Trump supporter: ‘Why do liberals think Trump supporters are stupid?’ THE SERIOUS ANSWER: Here’s what the majority of anti-Trump voters honestly feel about Trump supporters en masse: That when you saw a man who had owned a fraudulent University, intent on scamming poor people, you thought "Fine." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/onpolitics/2018/04/10/trump-university-settlement-judge-finalized/502387002/) That when you saw a man who had made it his business practice to stiff his creditors, you said, "Okay." ( https://www.thedailybeast.com/trump-hotel-paid-millions-in-fines-for-unpaid-work?fbclid=IwAR3HrrHWioVilc1-gUoxLZNVyyrl1eeZJ28tEkZ8CG0UrTk5BVBFF8yKDuQ) That when you heard him proudly brag about his own history of sexual abuse, you said, "No problem." (https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/list-trumps-accusers-allegations-sexual-misconduct/story?id=51956410) That when he made up stories about seeing Muslim-Americans in the thousands cheering the destruction of the World Trade Center, you said, "Not an issue." (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/fact-checker/wp/2015/11/22/donald-trumps-outrageous-claim-that-thousands-of-new-jersey-muslims-celebrated-the-911-attacks/) That when you saw him brag that he could shoot a man on Fifth Avenue and you wouldn't care, you exclaimed, "He sure knows me." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2019/10/23/president-donald-trump-could-shoot-someone-without-prosecution/4073405002/) That when you heard him relating a story of an elderly guest of his country club, an 80-year old man, who fell off a stage and hit his head, to Trump replied: “‘Oh my God, that’s disgusting,’ and I turned away. I couldn’t—you know, he was right in front of me, and I turned away. I didn’t want to touch him. He was bleeding all over the place. And I felt terrible, because it was a beautiful white marble floor, and now it had changed color. Became very red.” You said, "That's cool!" (https://www.gq.com/story/donald-trump-howard-stern-story) That when you saw him mock the disabled, you thought it was the funniest thing you ever saw. (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2016-election/new-york-times-slams-donald-trump-after-he-appears-mock-n470016) That when you heard him brag that he doesn't read books, you said, "Well, who has time?" (https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2018/01/americas-first-post-text-president/549794/) That when the Central Park Five were compensated as innocent men convicted of a crime they didn't commit, and he angrily said that they should still be in prison, you said, "That makes sense." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2019/06/19/what-trump-has-said-central-park-five/1501321001/) That when you heard him tell his supporters to beat up protesters and that he would hire attorneys, you thought, "Yes!" (https://www.latimes.com/politics/la-na-trump-campaign-protests-20160313-story.html) That when you heard him tell one rally to confiscate a man's coat before throwing him out into the freezing cold, you said, "What a great guy!" (https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/donald-trump-orders-protester-s-coat-is-confiscated-and-he-is-sent-into-the-cold-a6802756.html) That you have watched the parade of neo-Nazis and white supremacists with whom he curries favor, while refusing to condemn outright Nazis, and you have said, "Thumbs up!" (https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2018/08/why-cant-trump-just-condemn-nazis/567320/) That you hear him unable to talk to foreign dignitaries without insulting their countries and demanding that they praise his electoral win, you said, "That's the way I want my President to be." (https://www.huffpost.com/entry/trump-insult-foreign-countries-leaders_n_59dd2769e4b0b26332e76d57) That you have watched him remove expertise from all layers of government in favor of people who make money off of eliminating protections in the industries they're supposed to be regulating and you have said, "What a genius!" (https://www.politico.com/agenda/story/2017/12/29/138-trump-policy-changes-2017-000603) That you have heard him continue to profit from his businesses, in part by leveraging his position as President, to the point of overcharging the Secret Service for space in the properties he owns, and you have said, "That's smart!" (https://www.usnews.com/opinion/articles/2018-03-05/how-is-donald-trump-profiting-from-the-presidency-let-us-count-the-ways) That you have heard him say that it was difficult to help Puerto Rico because it was in the middle of water and you have said, "That makes sense." (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/politics/wp/2017/09/26/the-very-big-ocean-between-here-and-puerto-rico-is-not-a-perfect-excuse-for-a-lack-of-aid/) That you have seen him start fights with every country from Canada to New Zealand while praising Russia and quote, "falling in love" with the dictator of North Korea, and you have said, "That's statesmanship!" (https://www.cnn.com/2019/07/02/politics/donald-trump-dictators-kim-jong-un-vladimir-putin/index.html) That Trump separated children from their families and put them in cages, managed to lose track of 1500 kids, has opened a tent city incarceration camp in the desert in Texas - he explains that they’re just “animals” - and you say, “Well, OK then.” ( https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/more-5-400-children-split-border-according-new-count-n1071791 ) That you have witnessed all the thousand and one other manifestations of corruption and low moral character and outright animalistic rudeness and contempt for you, the working American voter, and you still show up grinning and wearing your MAGA hats and threatening to beat up anybody who says otherwise. (https://www.americanprogress.org/article/confronting-cost-trumps-corruption-american-families/) What you don't get, Trump supporters, is that our succumbing to frustration and shaking our heads, thinking of you as stupid, may very well be wrong and unhelpful, but it's also...hear me...charitable. Because if you're NOT stupid, we must turn to other explanations, and most of them are less flattering. - Adam-Troy Castro
item.dc:creator: WalkGood

item.title: Many others will do the same
item.description: I just finished my 22nd year of teaching. I'm the sole income in my home. If I had any other option, I'd likely be out the door, too. This is an unsustainable system.
item.dc:creator: mustbethedragon

item.title: Unconditional Love
item.description: This happened a few months ago, and she is actually now my fiancee. Happy Pride, y'all! I'm editing to add this: please don't comment "You could leave them if you wanted to". I know that. I am not with her because I feel required to be. I wanted to marry this person and them transitioning didn't change that . Our relationship is airtight and incredibly wholesome and healthy. 2nd edit: Thanks for the accolade!! 3: ANOTHER accolade‽ You guuuuuuuuys!
item.dc:creator: FrecklesIRL

item.description: Get well soon. Tough to smile when he's going through so much.

item.title: Actor from Star Trek, which has been championing diversity since 1966, can't believe we're still having to talk about this @#$%.
item.dc:creator: zombywoof68

item.title: No one cares. But I feel it needs to be heard.
item.description: “We’d do anything to protect our kids” “okay pick policies that might raise your taxes” “NEVER” it’s really starting to get old and I’m so tired and literally nothing changes. I’m scared for my kid, and none of this is helping that. The most frustrating part: husband is pro gun. When I asked him if he still was he basically said he’s scared to give them up because republicans/cops could still come for us and in situations like Ukraine they couldn’t distribute guns fast enough. Imagine being so scared in your own country that you won’t even consider giving up weapons to stop children being hunted for sport. Idk. I just feel like I’m sitting here screaming and no one cares. This is like the fifth gigantic traumatizing event I’ve suffered through because Texas and/or the US sucks (I grew up going to Uvalde all the time so this hits really hard, and also worked in disaster relief, huge yikes) and I am sick of being called selfish by my family when I talk about moving to a country where CHILDREN DONT DIE.
item.dc:creator: kirbyvictorious

item.title: JFC America
item.description: Every time I think we've reached a new low I see shit like this and just get that sinking feeling. And yes, this is real: https://www.thedailybeast.com/the-supreme-court-just-said-in-in-shinn-v-ramirez-that-evidence-of-innocence-is-not-enough The vote was 6-3. Edit: A couple people have mentioned this so I do want to give a content warning for the article above. It contains an extremely graphic and upsetting description of the crime that the defendant is being accused of. Although I really think there is insufficient evidence that the defendant is the one who committed this crime, it was still a deeply fucked up thing. Victims deserve justice, especially in cases like this. One of the worst aspects of this ruling is that it will ensure that innocent people will be put in jail with no real evidence, while actual perpetrators walk free.

item.title: “You don’t know what you’re talking about”
item.description: Calling it combined ignorance and hatred and telling the man he doesn’t know what he’s talking about. This man is clearly desperate to get his point across but it’s utterly useless. This ‘man’, Ted Cruz, is a blight on our country, and he MUST be voted out. Apologies for TikTok **Link with sound** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rm2H62XgNG0
item.dc:creator: CaptBuckMinster

item.title: Since February 2022 , I have…
item.description: It’s the second job interview out of 98 applications sent. Wish me luck y’all! FP Edit: I feel that the interview turned out well. What was expected to be a 30 minute interview lasted 53 minutes. The HR person said it was “a fun interview”. Is there anyone in HR out there who can tell me if I did well or not from this? I’m expecting to hear back from them in 10 business days. Edit too: I meant to say “I have an interview tomorrow”.
item.dc:creator: BanditHeelerSayingOhBiscuits

item.title: The landing can use some work
item.dc:creator: charizardkitty

item.title: 2nd time in 2 weeks I found a scared kitten in the middle of the highway
item.description:
I found this one in the middle of the interstate. He was so far in the roadway that my only option was to straddle him with my car to avoid hitting him. He was cowered in a puddle of what I assume was his own urine from fear. Poor thing can't keep its eyes open. Wouldn't stop trembling. Has an injury to its nose and mouth to be evaluated by a vet.
I was on a 4 hour drive when I found it (I think it's a him, but haven't gotten close enough to say for sure). His mouth is a little swollen but eyes and pupils are reactive. Was given water and a small amount of wet food.
Small amount of blood on his face. But his spirit is improving. Send good vibes for this little guy! We have a vet appointment later
This is Melodie, the first kitten I found in the middle of a local highway during rush hour. This is her shortly after I rescued her. She was uninjured when I found her and was given a clean bill of health. She is so sweet and playful. As soon as I found the other kitten on our trip, she immediately started taking care of him. What a sweetheart!
She is doing great and is happily integrated into my family.
item.dc:creator: literalcatlady
